seed: longing vs resolve; repair_vs_damage; mirror_broken; pulse; barcode against broken_dots; lit_edges; contained_vs_overflowing; thread; serenity_urgency | abstract variation request 230; target batch count 500
title: Tension of Repair
reasoning: This composition reflects the emotional tension between longing and resolve, using a palette that juxtaposes muted purple and deep blue against bright yellows and oranges. The ‘broken pixels’ are strategically placed to create a sense of interruption within a smooth pattern that suggests both repair and damage. The main contrast occurs at the center, where warm colors collide with deeper tones, resembling a pulse amidst quiet spaces. The layout symbolizes the distance and closeness of emotional states, while the 'mirror' pattern suggests the reflective quality of the longing. Meanwhile, the 'grid' provides a structured sense of resolve, offering a rhythmic balance throughout the artwork. Overall, the chaotic yet contained arrangement embodies the struggle between serenity and urgency, capturing a narrative of unresolved emotions yet striving for repair and clarity.

layout.name: scattered_repair
layout.description: Broken marks are patched by brighter pixels that form an incomplete repair path.

composition.name: near_vs_far
composition.idea: Close paired marks are separated from distant echoes by a wide quiet field.

referenceExample.title: Signal Inside Noise
referenceExample.pattern: one readable green path repeatedly interrupted by magenta static

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
